facts.text: Lauderhill’s name sprang from a playful debate where William Safire quipped that “Sunnydale” sounded like a Brooklyn neighborhood, leading to the coined name Lauderhill.
The town began as a cluster of off‑the‑shelf homes originally sold at Macy’s department store, a concept that later spread to New York and Florida.
In 1970 the Inverrary Country Club opened, and its east golf course hosted the PGA Tour’s Jackie Gleason Inverrary Classic through 1983.
Lauderhill sits in north‑central Broward County and is a principal city of the Miami metropolitan area.
The city enjoys a tropical monsoon climate, with weather patterns similar to those found throughout the Caribbean.
Lauderhill’s early development featured homes that were once displayed at the “Typical American Houses” exhibition in Moscow’s American Exhibition.
